solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
2222A - A Wonderful Contest
First, consider the case where $$$k=1$$$. We find that this condition holds if and only if there exists $$$a_i=100$$$. Assuming $$$a_d=100$$$, then letting $$$x_d=k$$$ and the remaining $$$x_i=0$$$ satisfies the condition $$$k\in [0,100]$$$.
Furthermore, we find that when $$$k \gt 100$$$, letting $$$x_d=((k-1)\bmod 100)+1$$$ and having $$$\lfloor \frac{k-1}{100}\rfloor$$$ of the remaining $$$x_i$$$ as $$$a_i$$$ and $$$n-1-\lfloor \frac{k-1}{100}\rfloor$$$ as $$$0$$$ constructs the corresponding solution.
In summary, the necessary and sufficient condition for any $$$k$$$ to satisfy the problem's conditions is that there exists at least one $$$a_i$$$ such that $$$a_i=100$$$.
